questions: What changes has Anthropic made to trial credit policies or API key requirements in the last 30 days that could affect platform-managed credit flows?
What is the current status of Anthropic's pricing changes scheduled around 22 June 2026 that could impact Mythos-as-planner strategy?
Are there any known outages or changes to the Australian Business Register (ABR) API that could affect ABN lookup reliability in production?
What competing RestoreAssist or disaster recovery SaaS platforms have launched or updated their offerings in the last 60 days?

findings.question: What changes has Anthropic made to trial credit policies or API key requirements in the last 30 days that could affect platform-managed credit flows?
findings.claim: Anthropic announced on May 14, 2026 that Agent SDK and headless claude -p usage would move to separate monthly credit pools (Pro $20, Max 5x $100, Max 20x $200) effective June 15; however, on June 15 Anthropic confirmed the change was shelved and all usage continues drawing from existing subscription limits unchanged. Trial API credit policy is unaltered: new accounts receive a small free credit grant; no new key-gating requirement was introduced.

sources.title: Claude Credit Overhaul 2026: Anthropic Pauses the June 15 Change
sources.excerpt: On June 15, 2026, Anthropic confirmed the change was shelved. Agent SDK and headless usage continue drawing from existing subscription limits unchanged.

sources.title: Pricing - Claude API Docs
sources.excerpt: New users receive a small amount of free credits to test the API. Contact sales for information about extended trials for enterprise evaluation.

sources.title: Anthropic Ends Subscription Subsidy for Agents June 15: Credit Pool Replaces Flat-Rate Access
sources.excerpt: Starting June 15, 2026, Claude Agent SDK usage, the claude -p command, Claude Code GitHub Actions will no longer draw from standard subscription usage limits.

findings.question: What is the current status of Anthropic's pricing changes scheduled around 22 June 2026 that could impact Mythos-as-planner strategy?
findings.claim: Claude Fable 5 subscription access (free on Pro and Team tiers) ends June 22, 2026; post-22 June subscription terms have not been published. Separately, Claude Mythos 5 — the model relevant to a Mythos-as-planner strategy — is priced at $10/MTok input and $50/MTok output via the API (same as Fable 5) and is currently under limited availability via the glasswing program; its API token pricing is stable with no announced changes around June 22.

sources.title: Pricing - Claude API Docs
sources.excerpt: Claude Mythos 5 (limited availability via anthropic.com/glasswing): $10/MTok input, $50/MTok output. Claude Fable 5: $10/MTok input, $50/MTok output.

sources.title: Claude Fable 5 Pricing, Access, and Usage Limits: What You Need to Know
sources.excerpt: Claude Fable 5 is available to Pro and Team subscription users at no additional cost until June 22. Anthropic has not published exact post-June 22 terms for all subscription tiers.

findings.question: Are there any known outages or changes to the Australian Business Register (ABR) API that could affect ABN lookup reliability in production?
findings.claim: As of June 18, 2026, the ABR reports all systems fully operational — ABN System, ABR Web Services, and Identifier Search are green with no incidents recorded in the past two weeks (June 4–18). No API-breaking changes or deprecation notices are active; the only scheduled downtime is the Christmas/New Year closure (24 Dec 2026 – 4 Jan 2027).

sources.title: Australian Business Register Status
sources.excerpt: All Systems Operational. No incidents reported June 4–18, 2026. ABN System, ABR Web Services, Identifier Search: all operational.

sources.title: Scheduled site maintenance | Australian Business Register
sources.excerpt: ABR will close normal operations from midday AEDT Thursday 24 December 2026, resuming Monday 4 January 2027.

findings.question: What competing RestoreAssist or disaster recovery SaaS platforms have launched or updated their offerings in the last 60 days?
findings.claim: HYCU R-Cloud added Hyper-V and Proxmox VE 9.0 support with a real-time replication engine in April 2026; no direct RestoreAssist-category (insurance-claims-focused disaster recovery for Australian SMEs) platform launch was found across PR Newswire, BusinessWire, or major review sites in the April–June 2026 window.

sources.title: New Features and Innovations in the HYCU R-Cloud Platform | HYCU
sources.excerpt: HYCU recently launched protections for Hyper-V with HYCU R-Cloud including backup, disaster recovery, mobility, and cyber resiliency features.

sources.title: 10 Best Cloud Disaster Recovery Solutions In 2026
sources.excerpt: Best cloud disaster recovery solutions in 2026 are ControlMonkey, Acronis, and Druva. No RestoreAssist-class competitor identified.

open_questions: What specific post-June 22 subscription-tier terms will Anthropic publish for Fable 5 and Mythos 5 access — will Mythos 5 remain limited-availability indefinitely or transition to general availability with changed pricing?

Are there Australia-specific or insurance-claims-workflow SaaS platforms (direct RestoreAssist competitors) that launched or updated in April–June 2026 that did not surface in general DR SaaS review-site searches?
